irrigation.title: 💧 Drip Irrigation System
irrigation.intro: Drip irrigation is a method of watering plants slowly through pipes, valves, tubing, and emitters. Water is delivered directly to the roots, reducing evaporation and water loss significantly.

weed_reduction.title: Reduces Weed Growth
weed_reduction.desc: Water only reaches target plants, not weeds.

yield_improvement.title: Improves Yield
yield_improvement.desc: Provides consistent moisture for better growth.

soil_erosion.title: Prevents Soil Erosion
soil_erosion.desc: Slow application keeps soil structure intact.

fert_efficiency.title: Fertilizer Efficiency
fert_efficiency.desc: Nutrients are delivered directly to roots.

energy_saving.title: Energy Savings
energy_saving.desc: Operates at lower pressure than sprinklers.

pump.name: Water Pump
pump.desc: The heart of the system, providing pressure.

filters.name: Filters
filters.desc: Prevents emitters from clogging with debris.

valves.name: Control Valves
valves.desc: Regulates the flow of water to zones.

pipeline.name: Main Pipeline
pipeline.desc: Carries water to the field areas.

emitters.name: Drip Emitters
emitters.desc: Delivers water precisely to individual plants.

guide.step1: Set up the water source and filtration unit.
guide.step2: Lay out and install the main pipeline along the field boundary.
guide.step3: Connect smaller lateral pipes to the main line for each row.
guide.step4: Punch holes and install drip emitters near each plant.
guide.step5: Test the system slowly to check for leaks and uniformity.

calculator.note: * Calculated based on average regional crop water requirements and 60% efficiency improvement.
